sentences.original: It was evening.

sentences.text: Un sirviente estaba solo bajo la gran puerta de Rashōmon,
sentences.translationRu: Слуга сидел один под большими воротами Расёмон,
sentences.original: A solitary lackey sat under Rashōmon
waiting for the rain to clear.

sentences.text: Bajo la ancha puerta no había nadie más.
sentences.translationRu: Под широкими воротами больше никого не было.
sentences.original: Beneath the broad gate, there was not another soul.
Only,

sentences.text: sentado en una columna grande y redonda,
sentences.original: on a big round pillar,

sentences.text: de la que se había caído la pintura roja en varios lugares.
sentences.translationRu: с которой кое-где облупился красный лак.
sentences.original: from which the vermilion lacquer had peeled off here and there, sat one lone cricket.

sentences.text: Rashōmon estaba en la gran avenida Sujaku Ōji.
sentences.translationRu: Расёмон стоял на большом проспекте Судзяку Одзи.
sentences.original: Since Rashōmon was in the great thoroughfare Sujaku Ōji,

sentences.text: Parecía que debería haber dos o tres vendedoras con sombreros de paja anchos y hombres con gorras de ciudadano refugiándose allí de la lluvia.
sentences.translationRu: Казалось, там должны были бы укрываться от дождя две-три торговки в широких соломенных шляпах и мужчины в горожанских шапках.
sentences.original: it would seem that there should also have been two or three tradeswomen in wide straw hats and men in citizen's caps sheltering there from the rain.
All the same,
besides this single man,
there was no one.

sentences.text: La razón era que durante los últimos dos o tres años en Kiōto
sentences.translationRu: Причина была в том, что за последние два-три года в Киото
sentences.original: This was because for the past two or three years in Kyōto
one calamity after another--
earthquakes, cyclones, fires and famines--had followed each other in rapid succession.
Wherefore,

sentences.text: en toda la capital había una desolación extraordinaria.
sentences.translationRu: по всей столице царило необычайное запустение.
sentences.original: throughout the capital the desolation was extraordinary.
According to the old records,

sentences.text: las cosas habían llegado a tal punto
sentences.original: things had come to such a pass

sentences.text: que rompían estatuas budistas y muebles de templos.
sentences.translationRu: что разбивали буддийские статуи и храмовую утварь.
sentences.original: that Buddhist images and temple furniture were broken up
and,

sentences.text: con sus restos de laca roja, oro y láminas de plata,
sentences.translationRu: с остатками красного лака, золота и серебряных листков
sentences.original: with their red lacquer and gold and silver foil clinging to them,
were heaped along the streets
and sold for fire wood.

sentences.text: Con toda la ciudad en ese estado,
sentences.original: With the whole town in such a state,

sentences.text: por supuesto nadie pensaba en mantener Rashōmon.
sentences.translationRu: разумеется, никто не думал о содержании Расёмон.
sentences.original: of course no one took the least thought for the upkeep of Rashōmon.

sentences.text: Así que zorros y tejones aprovecharon el abandono
sentences.translationRu: Так что лисы и барсуки воспользовались запустением
sentences.original: So turning its abandonment to their profit, foxes and badgers
found habitation there.
Thieves abode there.

sentences.text: Y al final se convirtió en costumbre
sentences.translationRu: В конце концов вошло в обычай
sentences.original: And finally it even became a custom with men

sentences.text: traer allí los cadáveres sin dueño
sentences.original: to bring unclaimed bodies there

sentences.text: y dejarlos dentro de la puerta.
sentences.original: and leave them in the gate.
Whence,
when day had closed his eye,
the place was abhorred of all,

sentences.text: y nadie se acercaba a esa zona.
sentences.translationRu: и никто не приближался к той округе.
sentences.original: and no man would set foot in the neighborhood.
Instead,
swarms of ravens

sentences.text: que venían de quién sabe dónde.
sentences.original: from nobody knows where congregated there.
By day
the innumerable rout described a circle

sentences.text: y volaba graznando alrededor de las figuras decorativas en lo alto del tejado.
sentences.translationRu: и летала с криком вокруг декоративных фигур высоко на крыше.
sentences.original: and wheeled crying about the ornamental grampuses high on the roof.

sentences.text: Sobre todo cuando el cielo sobre la puerta ardía con el brillo del atardecer,
sentences.translationRu: Особенно когда небо над воротами пылало закатным заревом,
sentences.original: Especially when the sky above the gate was aflare with the sunset glow,
did they stand out distinctly
like a scattering of sesamum seeds.
Of course,

sentences.text: venían a comer la carne de los cadáveres en lo alto de la puerta.
sentences.translationRu: они прилетали клевать мясо с трупов наверху ворот.
sentences.original: they were there to pick the flesh from the corpses up in the gate.
However, on this day,

sentences.text: quizás por lo avanzado de la hora,
sentences.original: perhaps because of the lateness of the hour,

sentences.text: no se veía ni un pájaro.
sentences.translationRu: не было видно ни одной птицы.
sentences.original: not a bird was to be seen.
Only here and there

sentences.text: en los escalones de piedra desmoronados
sentences.original: on the crumbling stone steps,

sentences.text: donde el pasto crecía en las grietas,
sentences.original: where grass grew long in the crevices,

sentences.text: sus excrementos brillaban en manchas blancas.
sentences.original: their droppings shone in white splashes.

sentences.text: El sirviente estaba sentado en el peldaño más alto de los siete,
sentences.translationRu: Слуга сидел на верхней из семи ступеней
sentences.original: The lackey sat in a threadbare coat of dark blue on the topmost of the seven steps

sentences.text: con un abrigo gastado de azul oscuro.
Se tocaba un gran carbúnculo rojo en la mejilla derecha
sentences.translationRu: Он трогал большой красный нарыв на правой щеке
sentences.original: and, fingering a great carbuncle on his right cheek,

sentences.text: y miraba sin ver la lluvia que caía.
sentences.translationRu: и бесцельно смотрел на падающий дождь.
sentences.original: looked vacantly at the falling rain.

sentences.text: He dicho que esperaba que la lluvia parara,
sentences.translationRu: Я сказал, что он ждал конца дождя,
sentences.original: I have said that he was waiting for the rain to clear.

sentences.translationRu: но даже если бы дождь прекратился,
sentences.original: But even if it did clear,

sentences.text: no tenía nada en particular que hacer.
sentences.original: he had nothing in particular he wished to do.
Ordinarily, of course,

sentences.text: debería haber vuelto a la casa de su amo.
sentences.translationRu: ему следовало бы вернуться в дом своего господина.
sentences.original: he must have gone back to his master's house.

sentences.text: Pero cuatro o cinco días antes
sentences.translationRu: Но четыре или пять дней назад
sentences.original: But four or five days before,
he had been turned out.
As I have already said,

sentences.text: Kiōto estaba en una decadencia sin precedentes.
sentences.original: Kyōto was in unprecedented decay.

sentences.text: Y el despido de este hombre por un amo al que había servido durante años
sentences.translationRu: А увольнение этого человека господином, которому он служил долгие годы,
sentences.original: And the dismissal of this man by a master whom he had served for years

sentences.text: era solo una pequeña ola en ese mar de problemas.
sentences.translationRu: было лишь маленькой волной в этом море невзгод.
sentences.original: was one small ripple on this sea of troubles.

sentences.text: Por eso, más que esperar que la lluvia parara, era más exacto decir que este sirviente sin lugar a donde ir estaba perdido, sin saber qué hacer, refugiado en ese lugar por la tormenta.
sentences.translationRu: Поэтому точнее было бы сказать, что этот слуга, которому некуда было идти, не знал, что делать, и просто укрылся от непогоды в этом месте.
sentences.original: Wherefore, rather than that he was waiting for the rain to clear, it would be more to the point to say that, driven to shelter by the storm, this lackey who had no place to go was at a loss what to do.
Besides,

sentences.text: el cielo gris de ese día tenía un efecto considerable
sentences.translationRu: пасмурное небо того дня немало влияло
sentences.original: the gloomy sky that day had no small effect

sentences.text: en el sentimentalismo de este hombre del período Heian.
sentences.translationRu: на чувствительность этого человека эпохи Хэйан.
sentences.original: on the sentimentalism of this man of the Heian period.
The rain,

sentences.text: que había empezado a caer un poco después de la hora del mono,
sentences.original: which had been falling since a little after the hour of the monkey,

sentences.text: todavía no daba señales de parar.
sentences.original: still showed no sign of letting up.

sentences.text: Así que el sirviente seguía dando vueltas en su mente
sentences.translationRu: Так что слуга продолжал кружить мыслями
sentences.original: So the servant sat following a rambling train of thought

sentences.text: al único problema vital e inmediato:
sentences.original: on the one vital and immediate question of

sentences.text: cómo podría sobrevivir el día siguiente —
sentences.original: how he could ever manage to live through the morrow--

sentences.text: es decir, cómo podría hacer lo imposible.
sentences.original: that is, how he could ever do the impossible--

sentences.text: Y escuchaba sin interés la larga lluvia
sentences.original: and listened listlessly to the long rain

sentences.text: que golpeaba sin parar en Sujaku Ōji.
sentences.translationRu: без конца барабанивший по Судзяку Одзи.
sentences.original: that kept pounding down in Sujaku Ōji.
The rain, enveloping Rashōmon,

sentences.text: y producía un ruido fuerte que venía de lejos.
sentences.translationRu: и издавал громкий шум, доносившийся издалека.
sentences.original: mustered a rattling roar from afar.

sentences.text: La oscuridad bajaba poco a poco del cielo
sentences.original: Darkness gradually lowered in the sky,
and overhead

sentences.text: el tejado sostenía una pesada nube gris
sentences.original: the roof of the gate supported a heavy leaden cloud

sentences.text: en la punta de sus tejas que sobresalían en ángulo.
sentences.translationRu: на остриях своих черепиц, торчавших под углом.
sentences.original: on a point of its obliquely projecting tiles.
For the accomplishment of the impossible,

sentences.text: no quedaba tiempo de elegir un plan.
sentences.translationRu: времени выбирать план уже не оставалось.
sentences.original: there was no time left in which to choose a plan.
If he took time,

sentences.text: solo podía optar entre morir de hambre bajo un muro
sentences.translationRu: ему оставалось лишь умереть от голода под какой-нибудь стеной
sentences.original: he could but choose between starvation under some wall

sentences.text: o morir de hambre al lado de un camino.
sentences.translationRu: или умереть от голода у обочины дороги.
sentences.original: and starvation by some road.

sentences.text: Y entonces lo llevarían al piso de arriba de esta puerta
sentences.translationRu: А тогда его принесут на верхний этаж этих ворот
sentences.original: And then he would simply be brought to the loft in this gate

sentences.text: y lo tirarían allí como a un perro.
sentences.original: and thrown away like a dog.

sentences.translationRu: Если же он не выбирал никакого плана,
sentences.original: If he did not choose,--

sentences.text: los pensamientos del hombre seguían el mismo camino de siempre
sentences.translationRu: мысли мужчины шли всё тем же привычным путём
sentences.original: again and again the man's thoughts went over the same winding way

sentences.text: y llegaban siempre al mismo punto.
sentences.translationRu: и всегда приходили к тому же.
sentences.original: and arrived finally at this same place.

sentences.text: Pero no importaba cuántas veces aparecía ese «si»,
sentences.translationRu: Но сколько бы раз это «если» ни появлялось,
sentences.original: But no matter how often this 'if' came up,
it remained still in the end but 'if.'

sentences.translationRu: Хотя он и не выбирал никакого плана,
sentences.original: Even though he did not choose any plan,

sentences.text: tampoco tenía el valor de admitir claramente
sentences.translationRu: у него не было смелости прямо признать
sentences.original: yet he had not the courage to make the positive admission

sentences.text: lo que necesitaba admitir para resolver ese «si»:
sentences.translationRu: то, что нужно было признать, чтобы разрешить это «если»:
sentences.original: naturally necessary to the settlement of the 'if,'

sentences.text: que no le quedaba otra opción que convertirse en ladrón.
sentences.translationRu: что ему не оставалось ничего, кроме как стать вором.
sentences.original: that there was nothing for it but to turn thief.
He sneezed a great sneeze

sentences.text: y luego se levantó con dificultad.
sentences.original: and then got up laboriously.
Night-chilled Kyōto was cold enough

sentences.text: tenía una temperatura que hacía pensar en el consuelo de una hoguera.
sentences.translationRu: таким, что невольно думалось о тепле огня.
sentences.original: to suggest the comfort of a fire.

sentences.text: El viento sin piedad soplaba entre las columnas de la puerta
sentences.translationRu: Безжалостный ветер свистел между столбами ворот
sentences.original: The pitiless wind swept with the deepening darkness between the pillars of the gate.

sentences.text: con la oscuridad que se hacía más profunda.
sentences.translationRu: по мере того, как темнота сгущалась.

sentences.text: Y el grillo que había estado agarrado a la laca roja de una de las columnas
sentences.translationRu: А сверчок, цеплявшийся за красный лак одной из колонн,
sentences.original: And the cricket that had clung to the red lacquer of one of them
had disappeared.

sentences.text: El sirviente metió la cabeza en el cuello del abrigo
sentences.original: Drawing in his neck

sentences.text: y levantó los hombros bajo la camisa amarilla brillante
sentences.translationRu: и поднял плечи под ярко-жёлтой рубахой,
sentences.original: and lifting his shoulders high in the bright yellow shirt

sentences.text: que llevaba debajo de su abrigo azul oscuro.
sentences.original: which he wore under his dark blue coat, the lackey looked all about the gate.

sentences.text: Miró a su alrededor por toda la puerta.
sentences.translationRu: Он огляделся по всей площади ворот.
sentences.original: the lackey looked all about the gate.

sentences.text: Si encontraba un lugar fuera del viento y la lluvia,
sentences.translationRu: Если бы он нашёл место, защищённое от ветра и дождя,
sentences.original: If he could find a place, out of the wind and rain

sentences.text: lejos de la vista de las personas,
sentences.original: and free from the gaze of men,

sentences.text: donde pudiera pasar una noche durmiendo tranquilo,
sentences.translationRu: где можно переночевать в спокойном сне,
sentences.original: where he could pass one night in peaceful sleep,

sentences.text: estaría contento de descansar allí hasta el amanecer.
sentences.translationRu: он был бы рад отдохнуть там до рассвета.
sentences.original: there anyway, he fain would rest until the dawn.
Then fortunately

sentences.text: sus ojos cayeron sobre una escalera ancha, también roja,
sentences.translationRu: его взгляд упал на широкую лестницу, тоже красную,
sentences.original: his eyes fell upon a wide ladder, likewise red,

sentences.text: que subía a la torre de la puerta.
sentences.original: mounting up into the tower of the gate.

sentences.translationRu: Наверху, если там кто и был, —
sentences.original: Above, though there might be men,
they were but dead men after all.

sentences.text: Entonces, con cuidado de que la gran espada lisa que llevaba al costado
sentences.translationRu: Тогда, стараясь, чтобы большой меч с простой рукоятью, висевший у бока,
sentences.original: Then, taking heed lest the great plain-handled sword swinging at his side

sentences.text: no se saliera de su vaina,
sentences.original: should slip in its scabbard,

sentences.text: puso un pie con sandalia de paja en el peldaño más bajo de la escalera.
sentences.translationRu: он поставил ногу в соломенной сандалии на нижнюю ступеньку лестницы.
sentences.original: he planted a straw-sandaled foot on the bottom step of the ladder.
A few minutes elapsed.

sentences.text: En el centro de la ancha escalera que llevaba a la torre de Rashōmon,
sentences.translationRu: В середине широкой лестницы, ведущей к башне Расёмон,
sentences.original: In the middle of the wide ladder leading to the tower of Rashōmon

sentences.text: el hombre estaba agachado como un gato,
sentences.original: the man crouched like a cat
and, holding his breath,
took in the state of affairs above.

sentences.text: Un rayo de luz que venía de la torre
sentences.original: A ray of light shining from the tower
faintly illumined his right cheek.

sentences.text: Era la mejilla donde el carbúnculo rojo brillaba infectado
sentences.translationRu: Это была та самая щека, где красный нарыв, воспалённый, блестел
sentences.original: It was the cheek on which the festering red carbuncle gleamed
in his short beard.

sentences.text: Desde el principio había calculado tranquilamente
sentences.translationRu: С самого начала он спокойно рассудил,
sentences.original: He had lightly calculated from the first

sentences.text: que todos los que estaban arriba estarían muertos.
sentences.original: that everybody up there was dead.

sentences.text: Pero cuando había subido dos o tres peldaños,
sentences.translationRu: Но когда он поднялся на две-три ступеньки,
sentences.original: But when he had climbed up two or three steps,

sentences.text: parecía que alguien no solo había encendido una luz arriba,
sentences.translationRu: оказалось, что кто-то наверху не только зажёг свет,
sentences.original: it appeared that not only had some one above struck a light

sentences.text: sino que la movía de un lado a otro.
sentences.original: but that he was moving it to and fro.
This was at once made evident
by the dull yellow gleam
that danced in reflection on the cobwebs

sentences.text: que colgaban en los rincones del techo.
sentences.original: hanging in the corners of the ceiling.

sentences.text: Quien hubiera encendido esta luz en Rashōmon en esta noche lluviosa
sentences.translationRu: Тот, кто зажёг этот свет в Расёмон в такую дождливую ночь,
sentences.original: Whoever had struck this light in Rashōmon on this rainy night

sentences.text: seguramente no era un ser normal.
sentences.original: was surely no ordinary being!
At last,

sentences.text: moviéndose con pasos silenciosos como un lagarto,
sentences.original: stealing up with muffled steps like a gecko,
the lackey, crouching,

sentences.text: subió la escalera hasta el peldaño más alto.
sentences.translationRu: поднялся по лестнице до самой верхней ступеньки.
sentences.original: scaled the ladder to the topmost step.

sentences.text: Luego, tumbándose tan plano como podía
sentences.translationRu: Затем он лёг как можно ровнее,
sentences.original: Then lying as flat as he could

sentences.text: y estirando el cuello hacia adelante todo lo que le llegaba,
sentences.translationRu: вытянул шею вперёд насколько хватало сил
sentences.original: and craning his neck forward as far as it would go,

sentences.text: miró con miedo dentro del desván.
sentences.translationRu: и со страхом заглянул внутрь чердака.
sentences.original: he peered with dread into the loft.
He peered,

sentences.text: y el desván, como decían los rumores,
sentences.original: and the loft, as rumor had it,

sentences.text: estaba lleno de cadáveres tirados sin cuidado.
sentences.original: was full of corpses flung carelessly away,

sentences.text: Pero el círculo iluminado por la luz
sentences.original: but, the circle illuminated by the light

sentences.text: era más pequeño de lo que parecía al principio,
sentences.original: being smaller than had at first seemed apparent,

sentences.text: así que no podía saber cuántos eran.
sentences.translationRu: и потому он не мог понять, сколько их.
sentences.original: he was not able to judge how many there might be.
Only, he could make out vaguely

sentences.text: que entre ellos había cadáveres con ropa y sin ropa.
sentences.translationRu: что среди них были трупы одетые и раздетые.
sentences.original: that there were among them both clothed and unclothed cadavers.
Of course,

sentences.text: hombres y mujeres parecían estar todos mezclados.
sentences.translationRu: мужчины и женщины, похоже, были перемешаны вместе.
sentences.original: men and women appeared all to be jumbled up together.

sentences.text: Y como muñecos de barro amasado,
sentences.translationRu: И словно куклы из мятой глины,
sentences.original: And like so many dolls of kneaded clay,

sentences.text: esos cuerpos estaban esparcidos por el suelo
sentences.original: these bodies sprawled on the floor

sentences.text: con las bocas abiertas y los brazos extendidos
sentences.translationRu: с открытыми ртами и раскинутыми руками
sentences.original: with open mouths and out-thrown arms
in such confusion

sentences.text: que uno dudaba de que alguna vez hubieran sido seres vivos.
sentences.translationRu: что трудно было поверить, будто они когда-то были живыми существами.
sentences.original: as to make one doubt even that they had once been living beings.
Moreover,

sentences.text: mientras la pálida luz jugaba sobre sus hombros, pechos y partes más elevadas,
sentences.translationRu: пока бледный свет играл на их плечах, груди и выступающих частях тела,
sentences.original: while the wan light played on their shoulders, breasts and more elevated parts,

sentences.text: las sombras de las depresiones se hacían más intensas.
sentences.original: the shadows of their depressions were intensified,
and they lay in silence
like eternal mutes

sentences.text: que nunca habían conocido el habla.
sentences.original: that had never known speech.

sentences.text: Con el hedor de carne podrida,
sentences.original: In the stench of decaying flesh,

sentences.text: el sirviente involuntariamente se tapó la nariz.
sentences.original: the lackey involuntarily covered his nose.
But the next moment

sentences.text: su mano olvidó lo que hacía.
sentences.translationRu: его рука забыла о своём деле.
sentences.original: his hand had forgotten its work.
Excess of feeling

sentences.text: le había quitado casi por completo el sentido del olfato.
sentences.original: had almost completely deprived him of his olfactory sense.
For the first time

sentences.text: acababa de ver a un ser vivo agachado entre los muertos.
sentences.translationRu: он только что заметил живое существо, присевшее среди мертвецов.
sentences.original: he had just caught sight of a living mortal squatting down among the dead.

sentences.text: Era una vieja parecida a un mono,
sentences.translationRu: Это была похожая на обезьяну старуха
sentences.original: It was a monkey-like old hag
in a dark brown kimono,

sentences.text: baja, delgada y de pelo blanco.
sentences.original: short, skinny and white-headed.

sentences.text: Con una astilla de pino encendida en la mano derecha,
sentences.translationRu: Держа в правой руке горящую щепку сосны,
sentences.original: With a blazing splinter of pine in her right hand,

sentences.text: miraba fijamente la cara de uno de los cadáveres.
sentences.translationRu: она пристально смотрела в лицо одного из трупов.
sentences.original: she was peering fixedly into the face of one of the corpses.
From its long hair,

sentences.text: parecía ser el cuerpo de una mujer.
sentences.translationRu: можно было судить, что это тело женщины.
sentences.original: it seemed to be the body of a woman.
For a space,

sentences.text: el criado sintió seis partes de horror
sentences.original: the lackey, moved by six parts of horror
and four of curiosity,
forgot even to breathe.
To borrow the words of an old writer,

sentences.text: «el pelo de su cabeza y de su cuerpo se erizó».
sentences.translationRu: «волосы на его голове и теле встали дыбом».
sentences.original: he felt that 'the hair on his head and body swelled.'

sentences.text: Entonces la vieja clavó el trozo de pino en una grieta del suelo.
sentences.translationRu: Затем старуха воткнула щепку сосны в щель в полу.
sentences.original: Then sticking the pine splinter into a crack in the floor,

sentences.text: Tomó la cabeza que había estado mirando
sentences.translationRu: Она взяла голову, на которую смотрела,
sentences.original: the hag took the head at which she had been gazing and, just like an old monkey picking lice from its young,

sentences.text: y comenzó a arrancar los cabellos largos uno por uno.
sentences.translationRu: и начала вырывать длинные волосы один за другим.
sentences.original: began to pull out the long hairs one by one.

sentences.text: Lo hacía como un mono viejo que busca piojos en su cría.
sentences.translationRu: Она делала это, как старая обезьяна, выискивающая вшей у своего детёныша.
sentences.original: just like an old monkey picking lice from its young,

sentences.text: Los cabellos salían fácilmente con cada tirón.
sentences.translationRu: Волосы легко поддавались при каждом рывке.
sentences.original: They seemed to yield to her pull.
With every hair that came out,

sentences.text: el miedo en el corazón del criado disminuía un poco.
sentences.translationRu: страх в сердце слуги немного уменьшался.
sentences.original: the dread seemed to depart appreciably from the heart of the lackey.
And at the same time,

sentences.text: un odio intenso hacia la vieja crecía en su interior.
sentences.translationRu: в нём нарастала сильная ненависть к старухе.
sentences.original: intense hatred of the old hag was little by little engendered.
No,

sentences.translationRu: пожалуй, это была не только ненависть

sentences.original: 'of the old hag' may not be just the right words.
Rather his antipathy to all evil

sentences.text: hacia todo el mal del mundo,

sentences.original: grew stronger every minute.
If some one at that time had broached afresh the question which this man had been considering under the gate a little while before,
whether he should starve or turn thief,
in all likelihood he would have unhesitatingly chosen starvation.

sentences.text: Así ardía su odio al mal,
sentences.translationRu: Так пылала его ненависть ко злу —
sentences.original: Thus fiercely,
like the splint of pine

sentences.text: que la vieja había clavado en el suelo.
sentences.original: the old hag had stuck in the floor, blazed up this man's detestation of evil.
The lackey, of course, did not know

sentences.text: por qué la vieja arrancaba el pelo
sentences.original: why the old hag was pulling out the hair
of the dead.

sentences.text: Por eso tampoco podía juzgar racionalmente
sentences.translationRu: Поэтому он не мог рационально судить о том,
sentences.original: Consequently, he did not know, rationally,

sentences.text: si su acción era buena o mala.
sentences.translationRu: было ли её поведение добрым или злым.
sentences.original: whether her conduct should be set down as good or evil.
But to him
the pulling of hair from the heads of the dead
on that rainy night
up in Rashōmon

sentences.text: era sin duda un crimen imperdonable.
sentences.original: was, on the face of it, an unpardonable crime.
Naturally he had already forgot
that a little before
he had had half a mind
to turn thief himself.

sentences.text: Entonces apoyó bien los dos pies en el suelo
sentences.translationRu: Тогда он крепко упёрся обеими ногами
sentences.original: So, bracing his two feet firmly,
he suddenly sprang from the ladder

sentences.text: hacia el interior de la sala.
sentences.original: up into the room.

sentences.text: Agarró el mango liso de su espada
sentences.translationRu: Он схватился за простую рукоять меча
sentences.original: Then, grasping the plain handle of his sword,
he advanced with great strides
up to the hag.
Naturally
she was startled out of her wits.
With a glance at the lackey,
she sprang up
as if shot from a catapult.
'Wretch! Where are you going?'
cursed the man,
blocking her way,
Where are you going?
as she stumbled among the corpses
in a panic-stricken effort to escape.
All the same, she struggled to push him aside
and get by.
But peremptorily he forced her
back.
For a moment,
the pair scuffled in silence
among the corpses.
But from the first

sentences.text: no hubo duda sobre quién ganaría.
sentences.original: there was no doubt of the victor.
In the end,

sentences.text: el criado le agarró un brazo,
sentences.original: seizing one of her arms,
the lackey twisted it

sentences.text: y la tiró con fuerza al suelo.
sentences.translationRu: и с силой бросил старуху на пол.
sentences.original: and threw her violently down.

sentences.text: Ella era puro hueso y piel,
sentences.translationRu: Она была сплошные кости и кожа —
sentences.original: It was nothing but skin and bone,

sentences.text: como la pata de una gallina.
sentences.original: just like the leg of a hen.
'What are you up to? Look you, what are you up to?
Out with it!
If you don't speak,

sentences.text: o te va a pasar algo muy malo! —

sentences.original: you get this, see!'
And casting her away,

sentences.text: y de repente desenvainó la espada.
sentences.original: he suddenly unsheathed his sword

sentences.text: La hoja brilló con un destello blanco
sentences.original: and brandished the white flash of steel

sentences.text: ante los ojos de la vieja.
sentences.original: before her eyes.

sentences.text: Pero la vieja se quedó callada.
sentences.original: But the old hag held her tongue.
Her hands trembling,

sentences.text: y los hombros subían y bajaban
sentences.original: her shoulders heaving
as she gasped for breath,

sentences.text: Sus ojos estaban abiertos de par en par,
sentences.original: and her eyes so wide open

sentences.text: como si fueran a salir de sus cuencas.
sentences.translationRu: казалось, они вот-вот выскочат из орбит.
sentences.original: that it seemed the balls must burst from their sockets,
she persisted in her silence like a mute.
At this,

sentences.text: el criado comprendió claramente por primera vez
sentences.original: the lackey realized clearly for the first time

sentences.text: que la vida y la muerte de esa vieja
sentences.translationRu: что жизнь и смерть этой старухи
sentences.original: that this old woman's life and death
depended entirely upon his will.
And before he was aware,

sentences.text: esa comprensión apagó el fuego de su odio,
sentences.translationRu: это понимание погасило огонь его ненависти,
sentences.original: this realization had cooled the fires of detestation

sentences.text: que hasta entonces había ardido tan intensamente.
sentences.original: that up to this time had blazed so fiercely in his heart.
What remained was simply
that comfortable pride
and satisfaction

sentences.text: de haber terminado algo por completo.
sentences.original: that follow upon a piece of work wholly carried to completion.

sentences.translationRu: Затем он посмотрел на неё сверху вниз
sentences.original: Then, looking down upon her,

sentences.text: y le dijo en un tono un poco más suave:
sentences.original: he said in a slightly milder tone:

sentences.text: — No soy ningún oficial de la policía.
sentences.original: 'I'm no official from the police commissioner's office.
I'm a wanderer

sentences.text: que pasó por esta puerta hace un rato.
sentences.translationRu: который проходил мимо этих ворот совсем недавно.
sentences.original: who happened to pass under this gate a little while ago.
So you won't be tied with a rope
and arrested.

sentences.translationRu: Мне только нужно, чтобы ты рассказала,
sentences.original: All I demand is that you tell me
what you're doing up in this gate
at this hour.'
At this,

sentences.text: los grandes ojos de la vieja
sentences.original: the old hag's wide-staring eyes
grew all the larger,
and she fastened them intently
on the face of the lackey.

sentences.text: Eran unos ojos rojos y afilados,
sentences.original: They were sharp red-lidded eyes

sentences.text: como los de un ave rapaz.
sentences.original: like those of some bird of prey.
Then she moved her lips,

sentences.text: que casi se unían con su nariz
sentences.original: practically one with her nose
among the wrinkles,
as if she were chewing something.
A sharply projecting Adam's apple
slid up and down
in her skinny throat.

sentences.text: Y entonces de esa garganta salió una voz
sentences.translationRu: И тогда из этой глотки вырвался голос —
sentences.original: And at the same time, a voice

sentences.text: como el graznido de un cuervo.
sentences.original: like the croak of a raven came pantingly from that throat

sentences.text: La voz golpeó los oídos del criado de manera áspera.
sentences.translationRu: Голос неприятно ударил по ушам слуги.
sentences.original: and struck harshly upon his ears.
'I'm pulling out hair,

sentences.text: arranco los cabellos de esta mujer,
sentences.original: pulling out this woman's hair,
because I'm going to make wigs.'
The servant was disappointed

sentences.text: ante la respuesta tan ordinaria e inesperada.
sentences.translationRu: от такого обыденного и неожиданного ответа.
sentences.original: at the unexpected ordinariness of her answer.
And at the same time,

sentences.text: el odio que había sentido antes
sentences.original: the hatred he had felt before,

sentences.text: mezclado ahora con un frío desdén.
sentences.original: mingled with a cold disdain, crept back into his heart again.
And its manifestations probably transmitted themselves to the hag.

sentences.text: Pues todavía sostenía en una mano
sentences.translationRu: Ведь она всё ещё держала в одной руке
sentences.original: For still holding in one hand
the long hair

sentences.text: arrancados de la cabeza del cadáver.
sentences.original: she had pulled from the corpse's head,

sentences.text: Y comenzó a explicar su caso
sentences.translationRu: И она принялась объяснять свою ситуацию
sentences.original: she mumbled her case

sentences.text: en esa voz ronca y croante.
sentences.original: in the croaking voice of a toad.

sentences.text: Quizás era malo arrancar el pelo de los muertos,
sentences.translationRu: Может, вырывать волосы у мертвецов — это нехорошо,
sentences.original: To be sure, it might be wicked to pull hair from dead bodies,
for all she knew.
But these dead were mostly people

sentences.text: que merecían ese trato, en su mayoría.
sentences.translationRu: которые в большинстве своём заслуживали подобного обращения.
sentences.original: who could well be treated in such a way.
For instance,

sentences.text: esta mujer a quien le arrancaba los cabellos
sentences.translationRu: эта женщина, у которой она вырывала волосы,
sentences.original: this woman from whose head she had just been pulling hair

sentences.text: había cortado serpientes en trozos de cuatro pulgadas
sentences.translationRu: резала змей на куски длиной в четыре дюйма
sentences.original: had cut snakes up into four-inch lengths

sentences.text: y las vendía como pescado seco
sentences.translationRu: и продавала их как вяленую рыбу
sentences.original: and sold them for dried fish
in the military camps.

sentences.text: Si la epidemia no la hubiera matado,
sentences.translationRu: Если бы эпидемия не убила её,
sentences.original: Had she not fallen prey to the epidemic and died,

sentences.translationRu: она продавала бы их и до сих пор.
sentences.original: she might have been selling them yet.
What was more,

sentences.text: los samuráis encontraban ese pescado seco sabroso
sentences.translationRu: самураи находили эту вяленую рыбу вкусной
sentences.original: the samurai had found her dried fish tasty
and bought them all up
to eat with their rice.

sentences.text: La vieja no consideraba mala la conducta de esa mujer.
sentences.translationRu: Старуха не считала поведение этой женщины дурным.
sentences.original: The hag did not find the woman's conduct blameworthy.

sentences.translationRu: Если бы та этого не делала,
sentences.original: Since she must otherwise have starved to death,
she could not well have helped it.

sentences.translationRu: У неё не было другого выбора.
sentences.original: she could not well have helped it.
Therefore,

sentences.text: lo que ella misma hacía ahora
sentences.translationRu: и то, что она сама делала сейчас,
sentences.original: what she herself now did
could not be called bad either.

sentences.text: También ella tendría que morir de hambre
sentences.translationRu: Она тоже умерла бы с голоду,
sentences.original: Since this, too, must be done or she would starve,
it could not well be helped,

sentences.text: Y pensaba que esa mujer muerta,
sentences.translationRu: И она думала, что та мёртвая женщина,
sentences.original: and she thought this woman,
who well knew her dilemma,

sentences.text: la perdonaría por lo que hacía.
sentences.original: would surely forgive her for what she did.
Thus, by the large, ran
the old hag's explanation.
The lackey sheathed his sword and,

sentences.text: con la mano izquierda sobre el mango.
sentences.original: with his left hand on the hilt, listened in cold blood to her recital.

sentences.text: La mano derecha tocaba el grano con pus
sentences.original: Of course, his right hand was busy fingering the festering carbuncle

sentences.text: que tenía en su mejilla ardiente.
sentences.original: on his fiery cheek.
But as he listened,
a certain courage was born within him.

sentences.text: Era el valor que le había faltado
sentences.translationRu: Это была та смелость, которой ему не хватало
sentences.original: It was the courage he had lacked
under the gate a while before.

sentences.translationRu: И к тому же это была смелость,
sentences.original: And, moreover, it was a courage
tending to move
in just the opposite direction from the courage

sentences.text: a la que le había llevado
sentences.original: with which he had a little before
mounted up into the gate
and seized the old woman.
It was not only that he was no longer at a loss
whether to starve
or turn thief.

sentences.text: La idea de morir de hambre
sentences.original: His emotions were now such that the idea of starving to death

sentences.text: casi había desaparecido de su mente.
sentences.original: had been driven from his consciousness as well-nigh unthinkable.
'Really?
Is that true?'

sentences.text: le preguntó en un tono burlón
cuando la vieja terminó su historia.
sentences.original: When the old woman had finished her tale, he questioned her in a sneering voice.
Then advancing one step forward,

sentences.text: Retiró la mano derecha del grano
sentences.translationRu: Он убрал правую руку от нарыва
sentences.original: he suddenly removed his right hand from his carbuncle and,

sentences.text: y agarró a la vieja por el cuello.
sentences.original: seizing the hag by the collar, said,

sentences.text: — Entonces supongo que no me culparás
sentences.translationRu: — Тогда, думаю, ты не осудишь меня
sentences.original: 'Then I guess you won't blame me

sentences.translationRu: за то, что я стану разбойником,
sentences.original: for turning highwayman,
will you?
I, too, must starve else.'
Like a flash
he stripped off her kimono.
Then,

sentences.text: cuando ella intentó aferrarse a sus piernas,
sentences.translationRu: когда она попыталась вцепиться в его ноги,
sentences.original: as she tried to cling to his legs,

sentences.translationRu: он с силой толкнул её вниз,
sentences.original: he violently kicked her down
upon the corpses.
It was but five paces to the head of the ladder.

sentences.text: Con el kimono oscuro y marrón bajo el brazo,
sentences.original: With the dark brown kimono under his arm,

sentences.text: corrió en un instante escaleras abajo,
sentences.translationRu: он в мгновение ока сбежал по крутым ступеням
sentences.original: he ran in a twinkling down the steep steps

sentences.text: hacia la oscuridad de la noche.
sentences.original: into the depths of the night.
It was not long before
the old woman,

sentences.text: que había quedado tendida como muerta
sentences.original: who had lain for a space like one dead,
raised her naked body up from among the corpses.
Murmuring and groaning,

sentences.text: gateó con ayuda de la luz
sentences.original: she crawled by the help of the light
that still burned

sentences.text: hasta lo alto de la escalera.
sentences.original: to the top of the ladder.
And from there,

sentences.text: con sus cabellos blancos y cortos
sentences.original: with her short white hair
hanging about her face,
she peered down
under the gate.
Outside there was nothing but

sentences.text: que la noche negra y vacía.
sentences.original: black and cavernous night.

sentences.text: El criado ya se había marchado
sentences.original: The lackey had already braved the rain and hurried away
into the streets of Kyōto
to rob.
